findings.message: D5 decides to create an independent `pr-reviewer` capability and explicitly rejects putting PR-provider data under the existing `reviewer` entry, but this consequence says migration is only adding settings to the existing `reviewer` entry. That points implementers at the opposite config shape and can reintroduce the `deny_unknown_fields` problem D5 is avoiding.

The ADR claims provider/model literals will disappear from `.claude/commands/**`, but D4 still requires `.claude/commands/track/pr-review.md` to keep the structured provider set as `codex` only. As written, the implementation cannot satisfy both statements without either removing the required PR-review Codex constraint or violating this consequence.
